2604.13127.title: Graph Propagated Projection Unlearning: A Unified Framework for Vision and Audio Discriminative Models
2604.13127.paper_summary: The need to selectively and efficiently erase learned information from deep neural networks is becoming increasingly important for privacy, regulatory compliance, and adaptive system design. We introduce Graph-Propagated Projection Unlearning (GPPU), a unified and scalable algorithm for class-level unlearning that operates across both vision and audio models. GPPU employs graph-based propagation to identify class-specific directions in the feature space and projects representations onto the orthogonal subspace, followed by targeted fine-tuning, to ensure that target class information is effectively and irreversibly removed. Through comprehensive evaluations on six vision datasets and two large-scale audio benchmarks spanning a variety of architectures including CNNs, Vision Transformers, and Audio Transformers, we demonstrate that GPPU achieves highly efficient unlearning, realizing 10-20x speedups over prior methodologies while preserving model utility on retained classes. Our framework provides a principled and modality-agnostic approach to machine unlearning, evaluated at a scale that has received limited attention in prior work, contributing toward more efficient and responsible deep learning.
2604.13127.paper_summary_zh: 選擇性和高效地從深度神經網絡中刪除學習信息的需求，對於隱私、法規遵從和自適應系統設計變得越來越重要。<lb/>我們介紹了圖形傳播投影遺忘（GPPU），這是一種統一且可擴展的類別級遺忘算法，適用於視覺和音頻模型。<lb/>GPPU採用基於圖形的傳播來識別特徵空間中的類別特定方向，並將表示投影到正交子空間，隨後進行針對性的微調，以確保目標類別信息被有效且不可逆地刪除。<lb/>通過對六個視覺數據集和兩個大型音頻基準的全面評估，涵蓋包括CNN、視覺Transformer和音頻Transformer在內的各種架構，我們證明GPPU實現了高效的遺忘，相較於先前的方法實現了10-20倍的加速，同時保留了對保留類別的模型效用。<lb/>我們的框架提供了一種原則性和模態無關的機器遺忘方法，並在規模上進行評估，這在先前的工作中受到的關注有限，為更高效和負責任的深度學習做出了貢獻。
2604.13127.author: Shreyansh Pathak et.al.
